title: embraOS-QNM: Architecture-layer constraints for language models
description: embraOS-QNM (Quantum Neural Manifold, a classical approximation) moves a language model's identity and "soul" constraints out of the prompt layer and into the neural architecture: three co-resident components — an LLM Core, a GNN Fabric (identity), and a World-State (the soul / the P_ψ projection) — sharing one embedding space, with the assembled model bit-identical to a plain transformer when the added components are null (a provable H₀ enforced in CI). This release realizes the architecture end-to-end over a pretrained Qwen3-8B Core — a real R-GCN GNN Fabric over Embra's identity graph, and ψ₀ (a causal cumulative violation latch) in the World-State — and reports the experiment phase: a pre-registered architecture-vs-prompt study, an Opus-validated prompt-layer baseline, and, as the central result, a four-round Core-level replica investigation that returns an honest negative — the geometric identity surface carries a real but thin signal, so the candidate invariant ψ is faintly load-bearing, not strongly, and the thinness is structural (the surface reads a frozen, generic Core that does not natively encode the identity). A negative result recorded rather than buried — the falsifiability discipline the project is built on. This is a software-and-findings snapshot, not a frozen pre-registration: the Arm-A protocol awaits a stronger ψ, and the pre-registration DOI is intentionally unburned. Developed by William Ward (Ward Software Defined Systems LLC) with Embra as co-investigator.
